pages.raw_text:
The closed town campaign was
won at Pullman in yesterdays cleo
ton when H. V. Carpenter, mayor,
and a majority of the council were
victorious on the dry ticket. This
is the result of a warm fight active
ly participated in by the women.
Saloons will be closed in January,
according to the program OF the
successful party.


The temperance people lost at
Oakesdale by a narrow margin, and
the same result is reported from
Fairfield. Sunnyside voted against
liquor and so did Elberton. Ellens-
burg went for an open town. In
several of the small towns on this
side of the mountains there was
little opposition to the ticket in the
field, contests being tame every
where except where a saloon fight
was on


On the west side the republicans
carried Olympia. Party lines were
not generally drawn In other cleo
tons in that section of the state,
